input_text: Provide the necessay reagents for thе following transformations. Моrе than one step may be required. a) O=C1CCCC1 + {box_1} = CCNC1CCCC1, b) C1=CC=CC=C1 + {box_2} = OC(CC)C1=CC=CC=C1, c) CCCCO + {box_3} = CCCC(O)=O, d) OCC1CCCCC1 + {box_4} = O=CC1CCCCC1.

input.question_text: Provide the necessay reagents for thе following transformations. Моrе than one step may be required. a) O=C1CCCC1 + {box_1} = CCNC1CCCC1, b) C1=CC=CC=C1 + {box_2} = OC(CC)C1=CC=CC=C1, c) CCCCO + {box_3} = CCCC(O)=O, d) OCC1CCCCC1 + {box_4} = O=CC1CCCCC1.

gold.reference_answer: {box_1}= 1) CCN, 2) NaBH4; {box_2}= 1) CCC(Cl)=O, AlCl3, 2) LiAlH4; {box_3}= Na2Cr2O7, H2SO4; {box_4}= PCC.

input_text:
Provide а synthesis of the following product from е given starting materials (additional reagents will be necessary). Each step in your synthesis must be clearly shown, but
no mechanism is required. Starting materials: C#C, CC(C)CCBr, O=C1CCCC1. Product: OC1(CCCCC(C)C)CCCC1.

input.question_text:
Provide а synthesis of the following product from е given starting materials (additional reagents will be necessary). Each step in your synthesis must be clearly shown, but
no mechanism is required. Starting materials: C#C, CC(C)CCBr, O=C1CCCC1. Product: OC1(CCCCC(C)C)CCCC1.

gold.reference_answer: C#C + NaNH2 = C#C[Na]; C#C[Na] + CC(C)CCBr = CC(C)CCC#C; CC(C)CCC#C + NaNH2 = CC(C)CCC#C[Na]; CC(C)CCC#C[Na] + O=C1CCCC1 = CC(C)CCC#CC1(O)CCCC1; CC(C)CCC#CC1(O)CCCC1 + H2, Pd/C = OC1(CCCCC(C)C)CCCC1.

metadata.selection_note: Selected from benchmark_v1_0 because the task explicitly asks for a synthesis plan or route proposal, not forward product prediction.

input_text: Determine a synthesis of the following product from the given starting materials (additional reagents will be necessary). Each step in your synthesis must be clearly described in SMILES format, but no mechanism is required. Starting materials: OCCO, CCCO, O=C1CC(C=O)CCC1. Product: OC1(CCC)CC(C(O)=O)CCC1.

input.question_text: Determine a synthesis of the following product from the given starting materials (additional reagents will be necessary). Each step in your synthesis must be clearly described in SMILES format, but no mechanism is required. Starting materials: OCCO, CCCO, O=C1CC(C=O)CCC1. Product: OC1(CCC)CC(C(O)=O)CCC1.

gold.reference_answer: O=C1CC(C=O)CCC1 + OCCO, H+ = O=C1CC(C2OCCO2)CCC1; CCCO + PBr3 = CCCBr; CCCBr + Mg, THF = CCC[Mg]Br; CCC[Mg]Br + O=C1CC(C2OCCO2)CCC1 = OC1(CCC)CC(C2OCCO2)CCC1; OC1(CCC)CC(C2OCCO2)CCC1 + H2O, H+ = OC1(CCC)CC(C=O)CCC1; OC1(CCC)CC(C=O)CCC1 + Na2Cr2O7 + H2SO4 = OC1(CCC)CC(C(O)=O)CCC1.

metadata.selection_note: Selected from benchmark_v1_0 because the task explicitly asks for a synthesis plan or route proposal, not forward product prediction.

input_text: Provide a synthesis of the following compound from the indicated starting material and any other reagents. Starting material is COc1ccc(C)cc1. Product is COc1ccc(C(=O)N(C)C)cc1.

input.question_text: Provide a synthesis of the following compound from the indicated starting material and any other reagents. Starting material is COc1ccc(C)cc1. Product is COc1ccc(C(=O)N(C)C)cc1.

gold.reference_answer: COc1ccc(C)cc1 + KMnO4 = COc1ccc(C(=O)O)cc1; COc1ccc(C(=O)O)cc1 + SOCl2 = COc1ccc(C(=O)Cl)cc1; COc1ccc(C(=O)Cl)cc1 + NH2CH3 = COc1ccc(C(=O)N(C)C)cc1

input_text: The antidepressant drug tranylcypromine is a primary amine with the amino group on a cyclopropane ring. Propose how you would convert an enantiomer of 2-phenylcyclopropanecarboxylic acid (which is made from trans-cinnamic acid) to tranylcypromine. (Hint: If someone is asking you to rearrange your atoms, it is best to be courteous.). What reaction would you use to effect the first transformation written below? Trans-cinnamic acid is O=C(O)/C=C/c1ccccc1, 2-phenylcyclopropanecarboxylic acid is [H][C@]1(C(=O)O)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2, tranylcypromine is [H][C@]1(N)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2.

input.question_text: The antidepressant drug tranylcypromine is a primary amine with the amino group on a cyclopropane ring. Propose how you would convert an enantiomer of 2-phenylcyclopropanecarboxylic acid (which is made from trans-cinnamic acid) to tranylcypromine. (Hint: If someone is asking you to rearrange your atoms, it is best to be courteous.). What reaction would you use to effect the first transformation written below? Trans-cinnamic acid is O=C(O)/C=C/c1ccccc1, 2-phenylcyclopropanecarboxylic acid is [H][C@]1(C(=O)O)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2, tranylcypromine is [H][C@]1(N)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2.

gold.reference_answer: Simmons-Smith reaction. [H][C@]1(C(=O)O)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2 + SOCl2 = [H][C@]1(C(=O)Cl)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2, [H][C@]1(C(=O)Cl)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2 + NaN3 = [H][C@]1(C(=O)N=N#N)C[C@]1([H])c2ccccc2, [H][C@]1(C(=O)N=N#N)C[C@]1([H])c2ccccc2 + heat, then H2O = [H][C@]1(N)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2; [H][C@]1(C(=O)O)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2 + SOCl2 = [H][C@]1(C(=O)Cl)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2,  [H][C@]1(C(=O)Cl)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2 + NH3, then Br2 + NaOH + H2O = [H][C@]1(N)C[C@@H]1c2ccccc2.

metadata.selection_note: Selected from benchmark_v1_0 because the task explicitly asks for a synthesis plan or route proposal, not forward product prediction.

input_text: Alizarin yellow R is an azo dye that changes color from yellow to red between pH 10.2 and 12.2. Provide a synthesis of alizarin yellow R from aniline, salicylic acid (o-hydroxybenzoic acid), and any other reagents. Aniline is Nc1ccccc1, salycilic acid is O=C(O)c1ccccc1O, alizarin yellow R is O=C(O)c2cc(N=Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1)ccc2O.

input.question_text: Alizarin yellow R is an azo dye that changes color from yellow to red between pH 10.2 and 12.2. Provide a synthesis of alizarin yellow R from aniline, salicylic acid (o-hydroxybenzoic acid), and any other reagents. Aniline is Nc1ccccc1, salycilic acid is O=C(O)c1ccccc1O, alizarin yellow R is O=C(O)c2cc(N=Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1)ccc2O.

gold.reference_answer: Nc1ccccc1 + CC(=O)Cl + pyridine = CC(=O)Nc1ccccc1; CC(=O)Nc1ccccc1 + HNO3 + H2SO4 = CC(=O)Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1; CC(=O)Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1 + NaOH + H2O = Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1; Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1 + NaNO2 + HCl = [N#[N+]c1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1].[Cl-]; [N#[N+]c1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1].[Cl-] + O=C(O)c1ccccc1O = O=C(O)c2cc(N=Nc1ccc(N(=O)=O)cc1)ccc2O.

metadata.selection_note: Selected from benchmark_v1_0 because the task explicitly asks for a synthesis plan or route proposal, not forward product prediction.

input_text: Outline a sequence of reactions that would bring about the conversion of aniline into benzylamine.

input.question_text: Outline a sequence of reactions that would bring about the conversion of aniline into benzylamine.

gold.reference_answer: NC1=CC=CC=C1 + 1) NaNO2, HCl, 2) CuCN = N#CC1=CC=CC=C1; N#CC1=CC=CC=C1 + H2, Pd = NCC1=CC=CC=C1.

input_text: Propose a synthesis of 2-propylcyclopentanone from adipic acid, using any other reagents containing 3 carbons or fewer. Provide the structures of key intermediates. Mechanisms are not required.

input.question_text: Propose a synthesis of 2-propylcyclopentanone from adipic acid, using any other reagents containing 3 carbons or fewer. Provide the structures of key intermediates. Mechanisms are not required.

gold.reference_answer: OC(CCCCC(O)=O)=O + H2SO4, EtOH = O=C(OCC)CCCCC(OCC)=O; O=C(OCC)CCCCC(OCC)=O + 1) NaOEt (1 equivalent), 2) AcOH = O=C1C(C(OCC)=O)CCC1; O=C1C(C(OCC)=O)CCC1 + NaOEt, CCCBr = O=C1C(C(OCC)=O)(CCC)CCC1; O=C1C(C(OCC)=O)(CCC)CCC1 + KOH = O=C1C(C(O)=O)(CCC)CCC1; O=C1C(C(O)=O)(CCC)CCC1 + heat = O=C1C(CCC)CCC1.

input_text: Outline a synthesis of the following compound starting from benzene, toluene, or aniline. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. O=C(/C=C/C1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1)C2=CC=CC=C2

input.question_text: Outline a synthesis of the following compound starting from benzene, toluene, or aniline. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. O=C(/C=C/C1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1)C2=CC=CC=C2

gold.reference_answer: C1=CC=CC=C1CC1=CC=CC=C1 + HNO3 = CC1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1; CC1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1 + KMnO4 = O=C(O)C1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1; O=C(O)C1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1 + 1) SOCl2, 2) LiAlH(O-t-Bu)3 = O=CC1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1. C1=CC=CC=C1 + AcCl, FeCl3 = CC(C1=CC=CC=C1)=O; CC(C1=CC=CC=C1)=O + 1) LDA, 2) O=CC1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1, then heat = O=C(/C=C/C1=CC=C([N+]([O-])=O)C=C1)C2=CC=CC=C2.

input_text: Propose a synthesis of the following molecule from succinic acid. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. (Hint: A good synthetic route takes advantage of the symmetry present in the target molecule.) O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O

input.question_text: Propose a synthesis of the following molecule from succinic acid. You have at your disposal any reagents containing four carbons or fewer. (Hint: A good synthetic route takes advantage of the symmetry present in the target molecule.) O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O

gold.reference_answer: OC(CCC(O)=O)=O + C=CCO (2 equivalents) + TsOH = O=C(CCC(OCC=C)=O)OCC=C; O=C(CCC(OCC=C)=O)OCC=C + 1) NaH (2 equivalents), C=CCO, 2) HCl, H2O = OC1=C(C(OCC=C)=O)CC(O)=C(C(OCC=C)=O)C1; OC1=C(C(OCC=C)=O)CC(O)=C(C(OCC=C)=O)C1 + MeI (2 equivalents), K2CO3 (2 equivalents) = O=C(CC1(C)C(OCC=C)=O)C(C(OCC=C)=O)(C)CC1=O.
